name: PHP - Laravel
scope: A person with BASIC proficiency level in PHP - Laravel should be able to read and write controller and service code in a Laravel 10+/11+ application: define resource routes, implement controller methods that use Eloquent models with basic hasMany/belongsTo relationships, author Form Request classes for validation, write and run database migrations and seeders, use Artisan commands for common tasks, configure environments via .env and config files, apply Auth middleware to protect routes, return JSON and Blade responses with correct HTTP status codes, write unit and feature tests with PHPUnit or Pest using assertDatabaseHas, actingAs, and fake() helpers, and work with the Eloquent query builder for filtering, ordering, and paginating results. They follow Laravel conventions for directory layout and naming, use Composer to add packages, and can read and extend existing controllers, services, form requests, models, and tests in an established codebase.
long_scope:
Competency Scope
PHP - Laravel – BASIC Proficiency (≈ 1–2 years hands-on experience)

Purpose
This scope defines the knowledge, skills, and expected outcomes for a practitioner who delivers routine Laravel backend features with guidance, understands the framework’s MVC conventions, and applies foundational engineering practices within an established Laravel codebase. The individual can produce production-grade code for well-defined feature requests when guard-railed by reviews, patterns, and documented processes.

1. Laravel Project Structure & Request Lifecycle
• Understands the request lifecycle from route definition through middleware, controller, service/model, and HTTP response.
• Navigates the standard Laravel directory structure (app/, routes/, database/, resources/, config/, tests/) confidently.
• Reads and edits config files (config/app.php, config/database.php) and .env; uses config() and env() helpers appropriately.
• Runs and interprets common Artisan commands: make:controller, make:model, make:migration, make:request, make:test, migrate, db:seed, route:list, tinker.

2. Routing
• Defines web and API routes in routes/web.php and routes/api.php using Route::get/post/put/patch/delete and Route::resource/apiResource.
• Groups routes with middleware (auth, verified, throttle) and prefix/name namespacing.
• Generates and uses named route helpers (route(), redirect()->route()).

3. Controllers & Services
• Writes resource controllers (index, show, store, update, destroy) with correct HTTP status codes.
• Injects dependencies (services, repositories) via constructor injection or method injection.
• Delegates business logic to a service class; keeps controllers thin.
• Returns JSON responses (response()->json(), resource()-based) and Blade view responses with appropriate status codes.

4. Eloquent ORM & Query Builder
• Defines Eloquent models with $fillable/$guarded, $casts, and basic local scopes.
• Performs CRUD operations using Eloquent: create(), find(), findOrFail(), update(), delete(), save().
• Applies basic relationships: hasMany, belongsTo, hasOne; retrieves related models and creates/associates them.
• Uses the query builder for filtering, ordering, limiting: where(), orderBy(), paginate(), get(), first(), count().
• Understands eager loading (with()) at a surface level to avoid obvious N+1 in small scenarios.

5. Database Migrations & Seeders
• Writes migrations to create and modify tables (Schema::create, $table->string, $table->foreignId, nullable, unique, index).
• Runs migrations, rolls back, and uses a fresh database for testing.
• Authors seeders and factories for generating test or demo data; runs them via db:seed.

6. Form Requests & Validation
• Creates Form Request classes with authorize() and rules() methods.
• Defines validation rules (required, string, max, email, exists, unique, date) and returns 422 responses with JSON error bags automatically.
• Accesses validated data with $request->validated() and $request->safe().

7. Authentication & Middleware
• Protects routes with the auth middleware; understands Laravel Sanctum token authentication at the level of attaching sanctum middleware and issuing tokens.
• Uses the Auth facade to get the authenticated user (Auth::user(), auth()->id()) within controllers.
• Writes simple custom middleware that inspects the request and either aborts or passes through.

8. Blade Templating
• Creates Blade layouts, extends them with @extends/@section/@yield, and includes partials with @include.
• Uses directives (@if, @foreach, @forelse, @auth, @guest, @can) for dynamic content.
• Passes data from controllers to views; escapes output with {{ }} vs. unescaped {!! !!}.

9. Testing with PHPUnit & Pest
• Writes feature tests that exercise HTTP routes: $this->get(), $this->post(), $this->actingAs($user), assertStatus(), assertJson(), assertSee().
• Uses RefreshDatabase or DatabaseTransactions to isolate test state.
• Asserts database state with assertDatabaseHas() and assertDatabaseMissing().
• Uses Mail::fake(), Storage::fake(), Queue::fake(), and Notification::fake() to assert side-effects without sending real emails or dispatching real jobs.
• Generates test data with model factories.

10. Error Handling & HTTP Exceptions
• Uses abort(404), abort(403), and throws ModelNotFoundException/AuthorizationException to surface errors cleanly.
• Returns consistent JSON error shapes from controllers and form requests.

11. Composer & Package Management
• Installs and removes packages via composer require/remove; understands composer.json require vs. require-dev.
• Reads and follows package documentation to configure a service provider or publish configuration files.

12. Documentation & Collaboration
• Writes clear PHPDoc blocks for public methods and non-obvious logic.
• Participates in peer reviews; reads and extends code written by others following Laravel conventions.
• Breaks requirements into migration + model + controller + form request + test tasks; estimates effort with guidance.

A professional who meets this BASIC scope is dependable for well-defined Laravel features, follows established patterns, and lays solid groundwork for more advanced architectural responsibilities encountered at INTERMEDIATE level and beyond.
